fragments.content: Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) is a neurodevelopmental disorder characterized by persistent deficits in social communication/interaction across contexts (social-emotional reciprocity, nonverbal communication, developing/maintaining relationships) and restricted/repetitive patterns of behavior, interests, or activities (stereotyped movements, insistence on sameness, highly restricted interests, sensory hyper/hyporeactivity). Symptoms present in early developmental period, cause clinically significant impairment. ASD is heterogeneous, ranging from requiring very substantial support to requiring support. Prevalence ~1-2%, male predominance (4:1). Pathophysiology involves genetic factors (heritability ~80%, hundreds of genes implicated), altered synaptic function, atypical neural connectivity.

No pharmacological treatment addresses core ASD symptoms; medications target co-occurring conditions (irritability: risperidone/aripiprazole FDA-approved; ADHD symptoms: stimulants/atomoxetine; anxiety/depression: SSRIs). Evidence-based behavioral interventions: Early Intensive Behavioral Intervention (EIBI)/Applied Behavior Analysis (ABA), Naturalistic Developmental Behavioral Interventions (NDBI), Early Start Denver Model. Speech-language therapy, occupational therapy, social skills training address specific deficits. Educational accommodations critical. Parent-mediated interventions improve outcomes. Early diagnosis and intervention (before age 3) associated with better outcomes. Transition planning essential for adolescents/adults.

DSM-5-TR ASD criteria: Persistent deficits in social communication/interaction (all 3): social-emotional reciprocity deficits, nonverbal communication deficits, relationship development/maintenance deficits. Restricted/repetitive behaviors/interests/activities (≥2): stereotyped/repetitive movements/object use/speech, insistence on sameness/inflexible adherence to routines, highly restricted/fixed interests, sensory hyper/hyporeactivity. Symptoms present in early developmental period. Cause clinically significant impairment. Not better explained by intellectual disability or global developmental delay. Specifiers: with/without intellectual impairment, with/without language impairment, associated with known medical/genetic condition/environmental factor, associated with another neurodevelopmental/mental/behavioral disorder, with catatonia. Severity levels 1-3 based on support needed.
